description_long: This object is a large cured ham leg, mounted on a wooden stand with a metal clamp securing the shank. The ham displays a mix of deep red, pink, and white tones, with visible fat marbling and some areas of aged, dried exterior. The wooden base is rectangular and supports a curved arm that holds the ham horizontally, allowing for easy slicing. The metal clamp at the top is functional, with a looped handle for tightening or carrying. The ham's surface is textured, with some mold or aging spots visible, typical of traditional Spanish jamón or similar cured meats.
description: A whole cured ham leg with visible marbling, mounted on a wooden stand with a metal clamp and handle.
description_view_0: Front view shows the rounded end of the ham facing forward, with the metal clamp and handle visible at the top.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the full length of the ham, the marbled meat, and the wooden stand supporting it.
description_view_2: Rear view highlights the textured, aged surface of the ham and the sturdy wooden base.
description_view_3: Opposite side view displays the deep red meat, fat marbling, and the metal clamp securing the ham.
attribute_grill_marks: No grill marks are visible on the surface of the ham.
attribute_visible_fat_marbling: Moderate fat marbling is visible, with some areas showing concentrated fat near the edges.
